pack.name: Git
pack.description: Most-used Git commands for everyday version control
pack.author: sherpacli-community

commands.name: Initialize repository
commands.description: Create a new Git repository in current directory.
commands.use_case: Use to start version control in a new project.

commands.name: Clone repository
commands.use_case: Use to copy an existing project from remote.

commands.name: Check status
commands.description: Show working tree status in short format.
commands.use_case: Use before staging and committing to review changed files.

commands.name: Stage all changes
commands.description: Stage all tracked and untracked changes.
commands.use_case: Use to stage all local modifications for a commit.

commands.name: Commit staged changes
commands.description: Create a commit from staged files.
commands.use_case: Use to save a logical unit of work.

commands.name: View history graph
commands.use_case: Use to understand branch history and merges quickly.

commands.name: List branches
commands.description: List local and remote branches with tracking info.
commands.use_case: Use before creating, switching, or deleting branches.

commands.name: Create and switch branch
commands.description: Create a new branch and switch to it.
commands.use_case: Use to start feature work in an isolated branch.

commands.name: Pull latest changes
commands.description: Fetch and merge from tracking remote branch.
commands.use_case: Use to sync local branch with upstream before work or push.

commands.name: Push branch
commands.use_case: Use to publish local changes to shared repository.

examples.description: Push current branch and set upstream

commands.name: Stash local changes
commands.use_case: Use before branch switches or pull operations.

commands.name: Hard reset to HEAD
commands.description: Discard all uncommitted changes in working tree and index.
commands.use_case: Use only when you intentionally want to drop local changes.
